introduction:
A Death Certificate in Uttar Pradesh is issued by the Nagar Nigam, Nagar Palika Parishad, Nagar Panchayat, or Gram Panchayat under the UP Registration of Births and Deaths Rules. It is the official legal document recording details of a death.

Essential for insurance claims, bank settlement, property transfer, pension processing, and succession proceedings. UP uses the e-District portal, e-Nagarsewa, and the CRS portal for online registration.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Register within 21 days (free)
  • After 21 days: late fee + Registrar permission
  • After 1 year: District Magistrate order required
  • First copy FREE
  • Track at edistrict.up.gov.in or e-nagarsewa.up.gov.in
  • Helpline: 0522-2304706
documentsRequired:
Documents Required

Note: Carry originals; submit self-attested copies.
Proof of Death
  • MCCD from hospital
  • Hospital death summary
  • Cremation / Burial receipt
  • FIR (unnatural death)
ID of Deceased
  • Aadhaar
  • Voter ID
  • PAN
  • Passport
  • Ration Card
Informant Documents
  • Aadhaar of informant
  • Relationship proof
  • Address proof
Delayed Registration
  • Affidavit
  • DM order (after 1 year)
  • Non-availability certificate
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via e-District / e-Nagarsewa / CRS

UP provides online registration via e-District UP, e-Nagarsewa (urban), and the CRS portal.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit Portal
    Go to edistrict.up.gov.in, e-nagarsewa.up.gov.in, or crsorgi.gov.in
  2. Register / Login
    Create citizen account, verify via OTP.
  3. Select Death Registration
    Navigate to Certificate Services → Death Certificate.
  4. Fill Form
    Enter deceased details, cause/place of death, informant info.
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload MCCD, ID proof. Max 200 KB
  6. Submit
    Note Application Number.
  7. Verification
    Local Registrar verifies within 7–15 days.
Tip: Track application status in real-time on the e-District portal.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Register Offline at Nagar Nigam / Gram Panchayat

Visit the Nagar Nigam, Nagar Palika, or Gram Panchayat office.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit Office
    Hours: Mon–Sat, 10 AM – 5 PM
  2. Collect Form
    Get Form No. 2 — free.
  3. Fill & Attach
    Fill form, attach documents.
  4. Submit & Get Receipt
    Receive Acknowledgement.
  5. Collect Certificate
    Collect within 7–15 working days.
Processing Time: 7–15 working days.
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Status

Visit e-District UP.

Download Certificate

Download digitally signed certificate once approved.

Legal Use

Insurance, property, bank, pension, and succession matters.

Grievance

Call 0522-2304706 or visit local municipal office.
